rulings.Case Title: ANA MACDONALD VS. DUKE PARTNERS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Duke Partners, Llcs Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 18, 2019 line 1. DEFENDANT DUKE PARTNERS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication The Court will treat this motion as motion for Judgment on the Pleadings. Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings is granted with leave to amend to name the beneficiary under the Deed of Trust and the foreclosing trustee as defendants. The Court did not consider points and authorities filed by Plaintiff in opposition to Nationstar's motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication because the total combined brief by the Plaintiff would have been 25 pages constituting violation of CRC 3.1113(d). The Court further notes that Defendant's objections to evidence submitted in opposition are well taken. However, no ruling is necessary at this time as no proper relief in this action can be obtained by either party in the absence of beneficiary and foreclosing trustee. Trial date of July 29, 2019 is vacated. This case will be set on CMC calendar. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: FISHER DEVELOPMENT INC. VS. 2675 FOLSOM OWNER LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 18, 2019 line 4. DEFENDANT 2675 FOLSOM OWNER'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD Hearing Required. No later than 9:00 a.m. on April 18, 2019 moving counsel to provide a courtesy copy of a proof of service indicating personal service on client. If this motion is granted, it will be contingent on counsel obtaining a trial continuance in department 206. = (501/CFH)

Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 18, 2019 line 2. DEFENDANT DEBBIE NGOW'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend to address in good faith all issues raised by demurer. No substantive opposition filed. Plaintiff's attempt to file a second amended complaint without leave of Court is improper per CCP 472 and CCP 430.41. Under the statutory scheme an initial pleading may be amended without leave of court prior to filing of a responsive pleading; or if a demurrer or motion to strike is filed, on or before the opposition due date. Code of Civil Procedure does not allow amendment without leave of Court of previously amended pleadings (Second Amended Complaint); answer to First Amended Complaint by East West Bank is also on file. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 18, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANT JUSTIN COHEN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, notice and complaint sufficiently state the nature of the nuisance. = (501/CFH)
